reviews.review_msg: really enjoyed her class wouldn't mind taking her classes in the future.

Bad accents but it's not her major issue. She have a weird political mindset. Strong political view students can't understand her simple logic, weak political view students will influence by her weird values. Her PowerPoint is ok, but her lecture is kind of irrelevant to her PowerPoint. Don't understand why she can teach in fic. Worst course.

Ying-Feng is a lovely, understanding, and flexible teacher. She shows passion and interest in what she teaches. Whether you are interested in communication or not, her content is of broad interest. However, she speaks pretty softly; at first, it can be challenging to understand her because her accent is thick.
She is fantastic as a lecturer. Does not put any unnecessary burden on students. Clear and straightforward about the concepts. She is passionate.

This professor's voice in class is so low that she needs a microphone to help amplify her voice. Even so, it was difficult for me to hear what she said. Her English has an accent that makes it difficult for me to understand her lectures.

I put in all the effort, I spent at least 3 hours a day in the library, and I got almost perfect scores on my multiple choice midterms, but she never gave you top marks on my papers, I don't know why, I attended every class, but my participation grade was only half, please don't pick her
dont pick her

I think working on her accent would make a significant change on her teaching. Her accent is very strong and even though she tries to help students, sometimes is difficult to understand what she is trying to explain/say because of her accent.
Just don't pick her, if you want to have a perfect college life. You can't understand what she's thinking.
Worst professor ever! I even cant understand her because of her horrible accent. Terrible attitude, unclear feedback. DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS.

I took her class asynchronously and it was hard, unclear and miserable. I dont even know why I have this or that grade, there was no feedback, it was hard to communicate or understand her idea, everything is so clueless and unclear, or even bored, meaningless and hopeless. Should not take her, only if you want to read a lot and good guessing

Her exam is not hard but you basically have to put much effort to get a good grade since the exams worth 50% overall in the class. My paper has a decent grade and other assignments as well, but not good at exams. 50% IS TOO MUCH!
Hard to score in exams.

She is very good at explaining if you're struggling with something. Very understanding! I wish I had taken CMNS110 with her as well. She made CMNS130 seem very interesting and I even enjoyed the readings!Went from C in CMNS110 to A+ in CMNS130:)

Shes not a bad teacher. Her lectures can be kind of confusing at times coz she have a thick accent. Try to ask her about things you dont understand and papers before its due for more feedback. What's hard about her course is the fact that sometimes she asks vague questions that needs specific answers. But all in all not bad
Great Professor and she is a very friendly person!
Very good Prof! She knows exactly what she's talking about!
Terrible
Lecture is too weak and not helpful

Hard to understand because of the pronunciation. Lecture in typical Asia style teaching form. Very not clear grading criteria. Lectures are not inspiring. It seems like it's just copied and pasted from Google and Textbooks.

Too demanding assignments and hard grading but too little clarity with topics and delivery. Listening to her lectures was a very frustrating experience. Unnecessary use of difficult words in addition to pronunciation problems in her English :(

Lost my interest in CMNS after taking this course with her. Her English is often difficult to understand and very monotonous. She gives lot of info in lecture slides to compensate this but it does not inspire to learn more. Nice in person but lacks engagement skills in class. Take this course only if u have no choice.

had a little difficulty with her english but she's actually very knowledgable and ready to explain more. She is very caring and kind. The lectures are interesting and fun yet challenging. You need to put your effort if you want an A.

My inspiration to learn communication ended after I took CMNS130 -Ying-Fen' knowledge of English leaves much to be desired - Reading as homework is not always related to class topics. +She has many office hours and she is ready to help -not easy to get A / A+ because her test questions are not fully clarified.

Her teaching voice is a bit boring but she is actually very knowledgeable and willing to explain more of a problem for you. Her slides are informative and good enough for you to pass this course with her. A very nice prof. Her CMNS 110 is a bit easier than 130 so 110 with her could be a GPA booster!
She's is a great teacher with a enormous amount of knowledge, her lecture slides are very informative and that's all you need to get an A in her class. Moreover her class is very fun and interactive.
Best professor for CMNS110.
she is a good teacher and very knowledgeable. She is always available if you have ay doubts. Had a little difficulty with her english but she covers it up with good teaching.
Lecture is good but it's hard to obtain high grades
gr8
The best professor ever met. She is really great and super easy. If you wanna get an easy GPA boost, take her course please

She's an awesome prof. A genuinely nice person and is extremely, extremely intelligent/well-versed in all aspects of class material. An interest in political economy is recommended for this class, or you may as well shrivel up and die throughout the semester.

Very nice and knowledgeable. Great prof for CMNS 443. English isn't great but is always available for help to explain concepts further. Bit of group work but decent format. Interesting material. Could make certain concepts clearer in class from the readings. Loved her despite a few teaching flaws she had!

In response to others comments. Yes her English is a bit broken and she is soft spoken. She is very knowledgeable, but does take a bit of time to formulate her sentences. Non the less, in CMNS 443 she had a great outline and it was a very interesting course. I would recommend CMNS 443 with her.

The students that are rating her poorly did not take the time to listen & absorb what she was saying in class, or grasp the readings well enough. She does have broken English & it makes her lecture a little bit disjointed but she is very willing to help students understand concepts. I am far from an A student and I found this class was just fine.
Not recommended.

The presentations take all over the class, she barely says nothing in a 4 hours lecture. And the final term paper, she first said we are welcomed to choose topic by ourself, however, she has a strong censorship over the topic. And YOU have to email her your topic to get approved by her in order to begin. An denied topic is usually happens

Worst lecturer I have ever experienced in my university career. Incomprehensible English to the point where you wonder if shes actually real. Attending lectures is useless, teach yourself the whole course. She does not make a semblance of sense, and probably never will. Awful, awful professor.
Very difficult to understand her. Was useless going to lecture.
Really really really hard to understand what she is trying to say thanks to her terrible accents. I got nothing out of the lectures.

She speaks pretty soft and slow in lecture so it can get pretty dull. But she's super friendly and smart, so if you don't get anything asking in tutorial or office hour is useful. I didn't think I would like political economy but I actually learned a lot from her :)

Fen is a lovely person. She is intelligent and approachable. In tutorial she was great and I could understand her better. As a lecturer though, I found that her accent really limited my comprehension. As a result, I would not take another class with her, unfortuantely. She doesn't speak English strongly enough to lecture a class, they were brutal.

I had Fen for 348, 444 and 240.  She is a wonderful professor with a lot of knowledge about the political economy of communications.  A lot of students couldn't grasp the political theory involved and complained, too bad, not the easiest material to deal with.  She made the material easy to understand and and was always willing to clarify concepts.

Most ridiculous prof I've ever had. I attended most lectures and every single time I would wind up frustrated because I couldn't understand a word she was saying. I really feel like I wasted my money with this class.

Really hard to understand but has good slides that she posts on-line. I honestly didn't learn much though and the readings were completely all over the place and her lectures seemed to be vaguely related to the readings (I may not have been able to understand enough to make the connection though haha...)
Her English is pretty much incomprehensible, which makes taking notes in lecture extremely difficult. She seems passionate about the course, but she needs to work on her public speaking skills.

Fen is an amazing prof.  Yes, she is soft spoken but her English is very clear and understandable.  More importantly, she is highly intelligent.  She uses great material and is always available for her students.  She has a wide range of knowledge. Just an awesome experience.  Highly recommend her.
she's awesome!!

Can not speak english clearly.  You cant understand a word she says. Her assignment outlines do not make sense.  She does not have any exams so you dont have to do the readings only for the papers you use them.  Really nice but the worst prof I have EVER had (including highschool) and I'm in forth year.
